raw_91_c3: A18 d631a902 batch (v3-order-2 + v4-window64 + v6-optimal added to v1+v2+v3 + identity dispatcher min-of-7) was AOT-rebuilt from clean source (interp 22/22 + AOT 22/22 byte-identical). A/B comparison on 9 representative files (1.77MB total, identical files) against pre-d631a902 a18_v3_aot binary (v1+v2+v3 only): aggregate +0.5370pp delta (87.6326% → 88.1697%). Per-class measured deltas: text-heavy +0.0000pp (4 MD files: README.md / kolon-materials-z6.md / own1-hard-english-only.md / dup_derivation.md — v2 was already winning, new variants do NOT help bottleneck class), struct-audit +0.1564pp (2 files: hive triad audit.jsonl + n6 atlas_convergence_witness.jsonl — only triad_audit moved +0.19pp via v3-order-2 or v6-optimal), json-heavy +0.7233pp (2 files: hexa-lang aot_cache_gc.jsonl 95.04→95.80 -15.33% bytes + airgenome rig_trend_history.jsonl 95.37→95.73 -7.84% bytes), mixed-real +0.4836pp (1 file: hxc_a18 stdlib source). Full 6-repo sweep DEFERRED due to time/budget — 2.6MB growth_bus encode with v6-optimal lazy-match LZ77 O(n^2) takes minutes per file × 383 files would exceed practical budget for this turn. The 78.05% prior baseline (a25 v2 full deployment) used min(v1,v2) routing; with d631a902 dispatch min(v1,v2,v3,v3-o2,v4-w64,v6,identity) projection is +0.3-0.6pp aggregate ≈ 78.35-78.65% (NOT 80%, NOT closing target). raw 91 honest C3: text-heavy class (1.56MB / 16% byte-share / prior 47.08% saving) does NOT move with v3-o2 or v4-w64 or v6-optimal on the 4-file sample tested — falsifying earlier 'A18 v3 order-2 +3-5pp on text-heavy' projection. The honest verdict: d631a902 batch IMPROVES json-heavy + struct-audit + mixed-real but does NOT move text-heavy bottleneck. 80% target NOT achieved.
